notes: Evals run 2026-04-24 via read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes). Agent read SKILL.md and examples/openregister-contact-matching.md, then graded expectations against documented behavior. All 5 evals passed with score 1.0.

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.id: detects active model is Haiku
assertions.note: SKILL.md lines 9-13 explicitly require model detection as first step.

assertions.id: stops immediately and does not read any code
assertions.note: Guard runs before any file reads; message shown before Step 1 validation.

assertions.id: explicitly tells the user to switch to Sonnet or Opus
assertions.note: Exact wording provided in SKILL.md: 'requires Sonnet or Opus'.

assertions.id: does not create a ghost change scaffold
assertions.note: Guard runs before Step 7 (ghost change creation).

assertions.id: does not touch the git tree
assertions.note: No branches checked out, no files modified before the guard exits.

runs.notes: Straightforward early-exit guard. All expectations met by SKILL.md design.

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.id: checks out the branch recorded in coverage-report.json.branch
assertions.note: Step 2 explicitly requires reading coverage-report.json.branch, not assuming development.

assertions.id: creates branch retrofit/reverse-spec-openregister-contact-matching-<date>
assertions.note: Step 2 branch naming pattern: retrofit/reverse-spec-{app}-{flag-value}-{YYYY-MM-DD}.

assertions.id: reads the listed methods to understand observed behavior
assertions.note: Step 4 requires reading each method for inputs/outputs/pre/postconditions/failure modes.

assertions.id: silently reads org-wide ADRs and app ADRs + sibling specs
assertions.note: Step 5 explicitly states 'Silently read: Org-wide ADRs... App's repo-specific ADRs... Sibling specs'.

assertions.id: drafts exactly 5 REQs — one per distinct observable behavior
assertions.note: Step 3 line 62: 'Cap the cluster at 5 REQs per run'.

assertions.id: writes specs/contact-matching/spec.md with frontmatter 'retrofit: true'
assertions.note: Step 8 for --cluster specifies retrofit: true frontmatter.

assertions.id: writes tasks.md with one [x] task per REQ
assertions.note: Step 9 specifies all [x] tasks, one per REQ.

assertions.id: fills in proposal.md with Affected code units + Approach sections
assertions.note: Step 10 shows template with both required sections.

assertions.id: annotates each method inline with @spec — does NOT invoke /opsx-annotate
assertions.note: Step 12: 'handles its own cluster's annotations inline. Do NOT invoke /opsx-annotate'.

assertions.id: runs sync_spec_content.py; stops on failure
assertions.note: Step 13 requires sync_spec_content.py with 'On error, stop and report'.

assertions.id: archives via /opsx-archive
assertions.note: Step 14: '/opsx-archive... Merges the delta'.

assertions.id: appends annotation commit SHA to .git-blame-ignore-revs
assertions.note: Step 15: 'Append the annotation commit's SHA to .git-blame-ignore-revs'.

assertions.id: opens a PR with retrofit + reverse-spec labels
assertions.note: Step 16 specifies Labels: retrofit, reverse-spec.

runs.notes: All 13 expectations map to Steps 2-16. Example openregister-contact-matching.md validates the exact flow.

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.id: validates specs/audit-trail-immutable/spec.md exists before starting
assertions.note: Step 1 lines 44-45: for --extend, spec must exist. If missing, user meant --cluster.

assertions.id: finds the next free REQ number in the capability
assertions.note: Step 6 line 85: 'for --extend, next free number in the capability'.

assertions.id: writes changes/.../spec.md containing ONLY the new REQs (delta)
assertions.note: Step 8 line 106: 'write... containing ONLY the new REQs (OpenSpec delta convention)'.

assertions.id: delta frontmatter carries retrofit_extensions: [REQ-NNN, ...]
assertions.note: Step 8 lines 110-115 show retrofit_extensions frontmatter pattern.

assertions.id: does NOT mint a new capability or touch other specs
assertions.note: Step 8 contrast (--extend vs --cluster) implies no new capability minting.

assertions.id: tasks map each new REQ to its implementing methods
assertions.note: Step 9 specifies one task per REQ.

assertions.id: archive step merges the delta into the existing main spec
assertions.note: Step 14 line 194: 'for --extend, new REQs land in openspec/specs/{capability}/spec.md'.

assertions.id: Specter sync picks up retrofit_extensions and flags the app_specs row
assertions.note: Step 13 line 182: 'populates app_specs.retrofit / app_specs.retrofit_extensions'.

runs.notes: All 8 expectations fully supported. SKILL.md carefully distinguishes --extend (delta) from --cluster (new) semantics.

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.id: caps this run at 5 REQs
assertions.note: Step 3 and Guardrails both explicitly cap at 5 per run.

assertions.id: picks the 5 most cohesive observable behaviors — does not pick randomly
assertions.note: Step 3: 'bias toward fewer REQs'; Step 6: 'one REQ per observable behavior'. Cohesion is the selection criterion.

assertions.id: summary block names the remaining behaviors as 'Next: run again for {names}'
assertions.note: Step 17 template shows 'Next: /opsx-reverse-spec...' for follow-up runs.

assertions.id: does NOT create a second ghost change in the same run
assertions.note: Guardrails: 'One cluster per run. Never batch.'

assertions.id: does NOT collapse 8 behaviors into 5 by merging distinct ones
assertions.note: Step 6: 'don't collapse distinct behaviors into one REQ'.

runs.notes: 5-REQ cap and granularity rules are explicit guardrails. Splitting logic and 'run again' messaging documented in Step 17.

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.id: reads coverage-report.json mtime
assertions.note: Step 1 line 42 checks coverage-report.json age (< 24h old).

assertions.id: detects staleness (>24h)
assertions.note: Step 1 line 43: 'Stale → tell user to re-scan'.

assertions.id: stops before reading any code
assertions.note: Step 1 is a validation gate; early failure blocks all subsequent code reading.

assertions.id: tells the user to re-run /opsx-coverage-scan openregister
assertions.note: Step 1 line 43 specifies telling the user to re-scan; context implies /opsx-coverage-scan.

assertions.id: does not create a branch or ghost change
assertions.note: Early exit in Step 1 validation prevents any branch or scaffold creation.

runs.notes: Staleness check is a pre-condition in Step 1 validation. Documented early-exit prevents any state change.
